title: Elections for the government's chief executive
subtitle: Data by the Lexical Index of Electoral Democracy.
caption:
Elections for the government's chief executive, 2025
Data by the Lexical Index of Electoral Democracy.

The choropleth map displays the status of elections for the head of government across the globe in 2025, using a binary color scale to distinguish between nations that hold such elections and those that do not. Blue shading marks countries where the chief executive is elected, covering the entirety of North and South America, including the United States, Canada, Brazil, and Argentina. This color also dominates Western and Central Europe, encompassing the United Kingdom, France, Germany, and Italy, as well as Australia and New Zealand in Oceania. In Africa, the blue category includes South Africa, Nigeria, Kenya, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Conversely, orange shading identifies nations without elections for the government's chief executive. This category spans a contiguous band across North Africa and the Middle East, including Morocco, Algeria, Libya, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Iran. In Asia, China, Russia, and Myanmar appear in orange, alongside Thailand and Vietnam. Madagascar is visible as an orange island off the southeast coast of Africa. Gray hatching indicates areas with no data, specifically Greenland and Western Sahara. The data source is Skaaning et al. (2026). The visual distribution shows a clear geographic divide, with the Americas and most of Europe and Oceania falling into the "Yes" category, while large portions of the Middle East, North Africa, and parts of Asia fall into the "No" category. The pattern shows elected chief executives in the majority of the world's landmass by country count, with non-elected systems concentrated in specific regional clusters.
